content: We can reframe a single general reinforcement learning agent’s developmental trajectory as an implicit three-stage progressive reinforcement learning pipeline: Stage 1 (exploration) uses curiosity-driven intrinsic reward to build out episodic memory, Stage 2 (active filtering) prunes low-value memory traces to retain only high-information state-action data, and Stage 3 (fine-tuning) refines task policies on the curated memory buffer. This structured, progressive development replaces the need to train multiple nested agents or repeat full retraining for new downstream tasks.
